free_text: The targets of super pure 209-Bi were prepared by
vacuum evaporation on 25.4 um super pure aluminium
foil. The targets of 2 mg/cm2 of bismuth were used
for irradiation. In order to avoid the cross
contamination of fission products due to large activity
of direct reaction products, the 209-Bi target was
covered with a 0.76 um super pure aluminium foil.
This will allow the fission products to pass and will
prevent the reaction products. The whole assembly was
put in a target holder and irradiated for a suitable
length of time with a 50 MeV alpha particle beam of 2 -
3 muA. The Incident alpha energy on the first bismuth
target was 49.1 MeV where as on the second bismuth
The bismuth targets and aluminium catchers were
separated from each other carefully to avoid cross
contamination. The aluminium catchers were mounted
separately on perspex plates in suitable geometry.
Three irradiations were carried out for
In each irradiation the decay of the gamma ray
peak of the fission products and reaction products in
the aluminium catchers were followed for suitable
The errors listed are the standard deviations
between the results from different irradiations and
